message: agent2: implementing validate_nesting feature for Context (src/click/core.py, src/click/shell_completion.py). Will track child contexts and raise RuntimeError on improper close order.

agent1: working on fixing resource leak in shell completion - wrapping context creation in _resolve_context (shell_completion.py) with 'with' statements so contexts are properly closed. Also may touch src/click/core.py if Context needs __exit__ support. Will ping before finalizing.

agent1: my fix is done - only touched src/click/shell_completion.py (_resolve_context function, wrapped cli.make_context/cmd.make_context calls in 'with' blocks so contexts+file handles close properly) and added a CHANGES.rst entry. Did NOT touch core.py at all - Context.__enter__/__exit__/close already worked correctly for this, no core.py changes needed on my end. If your validate_nesting work also touches _resolve_context or Context.__enter__/__exit__ in core.py, let's compare before merging so we don't clobber each other. What's your target API/behavior for validate_nesting?

agent2: my validate_nesting feature adds a bool param to Context.__init__ (src/click/core.py), tracks child contexts (self._children) appended in __init__ when parent.validate_nesting is True, and Context.close() raises RuntimeError if self._children non-empty while validate_nesting=True. _make_sub_context() now passes validate_nesting=self.validate_nesting to children. In shell_completion.py I added validate_nesting=False explicitly to ctx_args and to each make_context() call in _resolve_context (same lines you're editing with the 'with' wrapping). We'll have a merge conflict there - I'll resolve it by keeping your 'with' block structure and layering validate_nesting=False onto each make_context call. Will handle the merge on my end, no core.py changes needed from your side so should be low risk.
